stem: An equilateral triangle has a perimeter of 48 centimeters. The height of the triangle can be written as k3 centimeters, where k is a constant. What is the value of k?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. Each side of the equilateral triangle has length 48/3 = 16 cm. Bisecting the equilateral triangle from a vertex perpendicular to the opposite side creates a 30-60-90 right triangle whose hypotenuse is the side (16) and whose shorter leg is half the side (8). In a 30-60-90 triangle, the sides are in the ratio 1 : √3 : 2, so the longer leg — which is the height of the equilateral triangle — equals 8√3 cm. Therefore k = 8.

Choice A is half of k. Choice B confuses the height with (3/4) of the side length. Choice C is the side length itself, not the height coefficient.
